stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (solar panels) yields a guaranteed return of $185K. Option B (wind turbines) depends on weather patterns: with probability 45-67% (uncertain), it yields $381K; otherwise it yields $58K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in weather patterns affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $185K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $203.4K to $274.4K. Point estimate: $238.9K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 39.3%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 39.3%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
